task.title: Enforce a per-invocation tool permission boundary
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/541 by defining a provider-neutral in-process tool protocol that requires caller-owned authorization before every adapter execution.
acceptance_criteria: Stable non-blank tool IDs and explicit maximum effects are visible to the authorizer with the exact JSON input.
Denial prevents adapter execution, allowance invokes once, and adapter failures retain their source chain.
The kernel supplies no implicit grants, retries, real external tools, provider integration, or persistence.
Focused tests, pinned verification, independent review, hosted gates, and squash merge succeed.

attempts.summary: Selected a minimal caller-owned authorization contract.
attempts.diagnostic: A model-owned or reusable grant would have crossed unresolved safety policy. The bounded contract authorizes one exact tool ID, declared effect, and input before one synchronous invocation.
attempts.patch: Decision issue 540 separated permission enforcement from concrete adapters, persistence, retries, timeouts, sandboxing, and runtime orchestration before execution issue 541.

attempts.summary: Established RED with pure in-memory permission tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused integration target failed with unresolved imports because vela_kernel had no tool module or permission protocol.
attempts.patch: Kept tests for blank IDs, exact request visibility, denial before execution, exact successful output, one-shot failure, and source preservation before implementation.

attempts.summary: Implemented the synchronous tool and authorization protocol.
attempts.diagnostic: The kernel now owns ToolId, ToolEffect, ToolRequest, PermissionDecision, ToolAuthorizer, Tool, invoke_tool, and typed errors. Every effect including Pure is authorized; denial short-circuits and allowed adapters run once without retry or rollback.
attempts.patch: Added crates/vela-kernel/src/tool.rs, exported it from lib.rs, added tool_permissions.rs integration coverage, and documented ownership, partial-effect limits, and non-goals.

attempts.summary: Passed independent and hosted review, then merged.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned just verify and four focused tests passed. Rust quality gate run 29826695513 succeeded, and CodeRabbit reported no actionable comments with all pre-merge checks passing.
attempts.patch: Pull request 542 squash-merged as 471c0cd6b195c3fab1db17d9abfaf8ddd37a7392 and closed issue 541.

outcome.summary: Vela now has an explicit caller-owned permission checkpoint for every in-process tool invocation, with denial before effects and source-preserving one-shot execution failures.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test tool_permissions # 4 passed
GitHub Rust quality gate https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/actions/runs/29826695513 and CodeRabbit review at https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/pull/542

lessons: Permission belongs to caller-owned policy, not the model or adapter being authorized.
The authorizer must see the exact ID, maximum effect, and input for the one invocation it decides.
Denial must occur before adapter execution; after allowance, partial external effects cannot be rolled back by a synchronous kernel wrapper.
A no-retry contract avoids duplicating unknown external effects and leaves retries as new caller decisions.
